task_objective: Optimize process-tree parent PID discovery by reading compact /proc/<pid>/stat files instead of scanning /proc/<pid>/status.
design_decisions: Kept the change scoped to the internal _read_ppid helper so _process_tree ordering, cycle protection, and fallback behavior remain unchanged.
Parsed /proc/<pid>/stat by finding the final closing parenthesis of the comm field before splitting later fields, avoiding naive whitespace parsing.
Preserved the existing return contract: unreadable, vanished, malformed, or non-numeric process metadata returns None and is skipped by _process_tree.
Used static regression coverage to prevent accidentally returning to /proc/<pid>/status reads.
results: Baseline validation before edits passed: ./run.sh uv run pytest reported 270 passed.
The new stat-reader tests failed before implementation because _read_ppid still opened /proc/<pid>/status.
Targeted stat-reader and process-tree tests passed after implementation.
Full service test file passed after implementation: 65 passed.
Full validation passed: ruff check, ruff format, ty, pytest reported 273 passed, pnpm typecheck passed, and pnpm verify passed after rerunning with network access for pnpm audit.
